name: Agentic RAG
description: Agents that dynamically decide what to retrieve, when to retrieve, how to reformulate queries, and whether retrieved content is sufficient — rather than executing a fixed retrieve-then-read pipeline. Agentic RAG can iterate on retrieval, decompose complex questions into sub-queries, and synthesize across multiple retrieval rounds. The boundary with standard RAG is the presence of a control loop.
contrarian_take: Agentic RAG papers frequently benchmark on single-hop QA datasets that naive RAG also handles well — the added complexity of the agent control loop buys nothing on those evals. The signal worth tracking is evaluation on multi-hop, cross-document reasoning tasks where the retrieval plan actually matters, and where latency and token cost of multiple retrieval rounds is honestly reported.
example_signals: IRCoT: Interleaving Retrieval with Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique through Self-Reflection
